headline: Cambodia first agent baseline. Canonical composite 12.5 / critical band confirmed. Dominant negative signals: Hun Manet authoritarian succession consolidation (continuing from Hun Sen 2023 handover); scam-compound industrial-trafficking complex (Amnesty Jan 2026: 53 active compounds in Cambodia; 100K-150K trafficked workers across SE Asia, Cambodia largest hub by site count); 2024 State Department Trafficking in Persons Tier 3 placement carryforward; HRW 2026 World Report Cambodia chapter documents pre-election crackdown, restrictions on civil society, opposition party dissolution patterns; lack of independent judiciary; press-freedom collapse. Math reconstruction clean (dimension means at 1.5 across all 8 dimensions yield 12.5 composite). Confirm at 12.5. No first-baseline boundary case (12.5 sits 7.5 points inside critical band; not near a band threshold).

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1.5 confirmed. A1 detection: state-level suffering-detection mechanisms minimal; civil-society space severely constricted post-2017 CNRP dissolution; press freedom collapsed (CamboJA, VOD shutdowns). A4 amplification: international monitoring (HRW, Amnesty, UN Special Rapporteur) provides external amplification; domestic amplification suppressed. Critical band consistent.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1.5 confirmed. E1 affective resonance: state-level acknowledgment of trafficking-compound victims, political prisoners, and labor abuse remains minimal. Hun Manet rhetorical posture on poverty reduction not matched by victim-acknowledgment operational pattern.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1.5 confirmed. AC2 proportionality: scam-compound enforcement remains episodic and politically selective (Amnesty Jan 2026: 53 compounds active despite government claims of crackdown); AC4 resource mobilization: state security apparatus prioritizes regime protection over civilian-harm response.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.5 confirmed. EQ2 distributional awareness: rural/urban inequities, Khmer Krom and other minority concerns, indigenous land-rights pattern documented by HRW and Amnesty as systemic; EQ4 inclusion: opposition political voice suppressed.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1.5 confirmed. B3 scope clarity: state-business boundary collapsed in CPP patronage networks; B5 consent: 2023 royal succession-by-assembly without electoral choice; CNRP dissolution removed primary opposition consent mechanism.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.5 confirmed. AB2 transparency: minimal; judiciary lacks independence; AB4 reparation: trafficking-victim and political-prisoner reparation mechanisms largely absent.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1.5 confirmed. S1 causal reasoning: state response to scam-compound trafficking remains tactically reactive rather than systemically structured (Amnesty critique); S3 distributional awareness: regional/ethnic inequities persist.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.5 confirmed. I1 consistency: Hun Manet 'reform' rhetoric inconsistent with operational continuity of Hun Sen-era CPP rule; I2 non-performance: gap between stated anti-trafficking commitments and 53-active-compound documentation.

key_evidence: Amnesty International January 2026 report: 53 active scam compounds in Cambodia; 100,000-150,000 trafficked workers across Southeast Asia with Cambodia as largest hub by site count; documented forced labor, beatings, torture, sexual violence.
Hun Manet authoritarian succession: 2023 handover from Hun Sen; CPP single-party-dominant rule continuing; family/CPP-aligned ministerial roles documented.
HRW World Report 2026 Cambodia chapter: pre-election crackdown patterns, civil society restrictions, opposition party dissolution legacy (CNRP 2017), press freedom collapse (VOD, CamboJA shutdowns).
US State Department 2024 Trafficking in Persons report: Cambodia at Tier 3 placement (lowest tier) — government does not fully meet minimum standards and is not making significant efforts.
UN Special Rapporteur on the situation of human rights in Cambodia: continued documentation of restrictions on civil and political rights.
Sam Rainsy and other opposition figures remain in exile or face legal proceedings; political prisoner cases documented.
Population scale: ~17M; first agent baseline establishment; high-importance never-assessed entity per scanner.
Math reconstruction clean: AWR/EMP/ACT/EQU/BND/ACC/SYS/INT all at 1.5 yield composite 12.5 (no discrepancy).

evidence.source: Amnesty International — Scamming the world: forced labor and trafficking in Southeast Asia (January 2026)
evidence.finding: 53 active scam compounds in Cambodia; 100K-150K trafficked workers across SE Asia; Cambodia is largest hub by site count; forced labor, beatings, torture, sexual violence documented.

evidence.source: Human Rights Watch — World Report 2026 Cambodia
evidence.finding: Anchor: pre-election crackdown patterns, civil society restrictions, opposition party dissolution legacy, press freedom collapse, judicial subordination.

evidence.source: US State Department 2024 Trafficking in Persons Report — Cambodia (Tier 3)
evidence.finding: Tier 3 placement (lowest tier): government does not fully meet minimum standards and is not making significant efforts.

evidence.source: US State Department 2024 Human Rights Reports — Cambodia
evidence.finding: Cumulative baseline: arbitrary detention, restrictions on political participation, opposition suppression, judicial subordination.

evidence.source: UN Special Rapporteur on the situation of human rights in Cambodia
evidence.finding: Continued documentation of restrictions on civil and political rights; international monitoring access narrowed.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction confirms 12.5. Critical band sustained. No discrepancy. First-baseline confirm at 12.5.

negativeSignals: 53 active scam compounds (Amnesty Jan 2026); 100K-150K trafficked workers across SE Asia with Cambodia largest hub; Tier 3 TIP placement; CNRP opposition dissolution legacy; press freedom collapse (VOD, CamboJA shutdowns); Hun Manet authoritarian succession (CPP continuity); restrictions on civil and political rights; judicial subordination; political prisoner cases.
positiveSignals: International monitoring access partial (UN Special Rapporteur, HRW, Amnesty); some Hun Manet 'reform' rhetoric (not matched by operational change); regional ASEAN engagement context; economic growth and poverty reduction continuing at population level (sub-threshold; not a compassion-behavior signal).
sector_context: Countries / Southeast Asia cluster. First agent baseline. Cross-reference: Myanmar (military rule, conflict floor, scam-compound regional hub), Laos (single-party, scam-compound regional hub), Vietnam (single-party, contrasting governance model), Thailand (transitional democracy, scam-compound transit), Philippines (electoral democracy, contrasting governance). Cambodia distinguished by 53-active-compound density (largest in region by site count) + Tier 3 TIP placement + post-handover authoritarian consolidation.
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with critical-band confirmation
Amnesty Jan 2026 scam-compound evidence absorbed into dimension scoring
HRW 2026 + TIP Tier 3 + State Dept 2024 evidence base integrated
open_actions_carried: Watch: any scam-compound enforcement scaling beyond episodic raids
Watch: Sam Rainsy and opposition political-prisoner case developments
Watch: UN Human Rights Council Cambodia resolution cycle
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
